The people were counted to demonstrate God's faithfulness in increasing Jacob’s descendants, so that they could be better prepared for the wars and conquest of Canaan, and to determine their families for the division of the land. It is said of each tribe that those who were able to go to war were numbered; they had wars before them, even though they currently faced no opposition. Let the believer be ready to resist the enemies of his soul, even if all appears peaceful.

We have here the sum total. How much was needed to maintain all these in the wilderness! They were all provided for by God every day. When we observe God’s faithfulness, even if his promise seems unlikely to be kept, we may take courage regarding those which still remain to be fulfilled to the church of God.

Care is taken here to distinguish the tribe of Levi, which, in the matter of the golden calf, had distinguished itself. Singular services will be recompensed by singular honors. It was for the honor of the Levites that they were entrusted with the care of the tabernacle and its treasures, in their camps and in their marches. It was for the honor of the holy things that none should see them or touch them but those who are called by God to the service. We all are unfit and unworthy to have fellowship with God until called by his grace into the fellowship of his Son Jesus Christ our Lord; and so, being the spiritual seed of that great High Priest, we are made priests to our God. Great care must be taken to prevent sin, for preventing sin prevents wrath. Being a holy tribe, they were not reckoned among other Israelites. Those who minister about holy things should neither entangle themselves nor be entangled in worldly affairs. And let every believer seek to do what the Lord has commanded.
